description: Target table for the bridge-backed reference campaign. It mixes toy-placeholder controls, derived-synthetic checks, and one stronger evidence-grade benchmark that is anchored to imported-geometry provenance rather than direct experiment. This is still not real-world physics evidence, but it is strong enough to separate control-study success from benchmark mismatch.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-1-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-2-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-3-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: fermionic-mode-1-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: fermionic-mode-2-ratio-control
targets.notes: Control-study target aligned with the historical toy reference table.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-1-ratio-derived-synthetic
targets.notes: Derived-synthetic check from the executable SU(2) toy model; useful for checking whether the structured-environment observable stays near the analytic reference.

targets.label: fermionic-mode-1-ratio-small-sample
targets.notes: Small-sample control using a Student-t tail model instead of a Gaussian one.

targets.label: bosonic-mode-2-imported-geometry-benchmark
targets.notes: Stronger benchmark tied to the imported-geometry side of the campaign. It is evidence-grade within the repository contract because it is not a toy placeholder, but it is still not an experimental measurement.
